chunk_summary: Guidance on breastfeeding twins or multiples and after cesarean delivery, emphasizing early stimulation, tandem feeding, safe positioning, and managing delayed lactogenesis due to cesarean birth.
learning_objectives: Understand strategies for breastfeeding twins or higher-order multiples, including tandem feeding and alternating breasts.
Learn techniques for breastfeeding after cesarean delivery, including skin-to-skin contact and incision-friendly positions.
Recognize the importance of monitoring infant weight gain and urine output to ensure adequate feeding.
Identify methods to maintain milk supply during mother–infant separation or supplementation.
key_facts: Mothers of multiples can produce sufficient milk with early stimulation and frequent milk removal.
Monitor each infant separately for weight gain and urine output, as feeding effectiveness may vary.
Cesarean delivery increases the risk of delayed lactogenesis; early hand expression (≥8 times/day) is critical.
Skin-to-skin contact in the operating room or recovery area supports breastfeeding initiation.
Safe breastfeeding positions after cesarean include football hold and side-lying to avoid incision pressure.
reference_quote: Early skin-to-skin in the operating room or recovery area and uninterrupted rooming-in are key. (Trainer’s guide: Mother’s Absolute Affection (MAA) programme)
